Drain a siphon pipeline

Source: R/run.R
pump_drain.Rd

pump_drain() runs the pipeline, pulling items and passing them to a callback function as they become ready. This is a memory-safe alternative to pump_run() suitable for infinite or long-running pipelines.

Usage

pump_drain(
  x,
  handle_fn,
  sleep_ms = 10,
  verbose = TRUE,
  on_error = "stop",
  backend = "main",
  timeout = NULL
)

Arguments

x

A pump object or a finite R object.

handle_fn

A callback function with signature function(id, data, ok) called for each completed item.

sleep_ms

Delay in milliseconds between polls when no item is ready.

verbose

If TRUE, show a text progress bar.

on_error

Default error handling policy for all stages that do not explicitly set their own on_error: "stop" throws on first error, "collect" delivers error items to handle_fn with ok = FALSE, "continue" drops failed items. Defaults to "stop".

backend

Default backend for all stages that do not explicitly set their own backend. Can be a backend object or one of "main", "mirai", or "future". Use parallel_backend() directly for fault-tolerant PSOCK execution (no string alias). Defaults to "main".

timeout

Maximum time in seconds to wait for completion. If NULL (default), waits indefinitely. If exceeded, throws an error. See the Details section of pump_run() for the cooperative-checking caveats, which apply here equally.

Value

Invisible NULL.

Details

Unlike pump_run(), draining an already-exhausted pipeline is a silent no-op, so daemon-style loops can re-invoke pump_drain() harmlessly.

Examples

results <- list()
f <- 1:5 |> pump(function(x) x * 2, backend = "main")
pump_drain(f, handle_fn = function(id, data, ok) {
    results[[id]] <<- data
})
#> 
  |                                                                            
  |                                                                      |   0%
  |                                                                            
  |==============                                                        |  20%
  |                                                                            
  |============================                                          |  40%
  |                                                                            
  |==========================================                            |  60%
  |                                                                            
  |========================================================              |  80%
  |                                                                            
  |======================================================================| 100%
print(results)
#> [[1]]
#> [1] 2
#> 
#> [[2]]
#> [1] 4
#> 
#> [[3]]
#> [1] 6
#> 
#> [[4]]
#> [1] 8
#> 
#> [[5]]
#> [1] 10
#>
